stem: A industrial engineering study measures the assembly time (seconds) for a sample of 20 factory workers. The sample mean is 214.7 sec with a sample standard deviation of 59.3 sec. However, 2 of the 20 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 18 observations have a mean of 203.5 sec and standard deviation of 41.0 sec. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 214.7, 95% CI = [186.9, 242.4]. Without outliers: mean = 203.5, 95% CI = [184.2, 222.8]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [184.2, 242.4]. Point estimate: 209.1 sec.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [184.2, 242.4].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
